title: Solar Radiation and Albedo Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive MicroSim demonstrating how surface albedo affects energy absorption, temperature, and climate feedback loops.

educational.topic: Solar Radiation, Albedo, and Climate Feedback
learningObjectives: Calculate the net energy absorbed by different surfaces and predict how albedo changes affect local temperature
Explain the relationship between surface albedo and energy balance
Describe the ice-albedo positive feedback loop and its role in climate change
Predict how land-use changes affect local and global energy budgets
prerequisites: Basic understanding of solar radiation and the electromagnetic spectrum
Understanding of the greenhouse effect at an introductory level

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Visual energy budget with surface type comparison and feedback loop animation
keyQuestions: Which surface type absorbs the most solar energy? Which reflects the most?
What is the ice-albedo feedback loop and why is it called a positive feedback?
How does replacing forest with a parking lot change local temperature?
Why is climate change accelerating faster in Arctic regions?
commonMisconceptions: Albedo only matters for snow and ice, not for other surface types
Positive feedback means the effect is beneficial, rather than self-amplifying
All dark surfaces have the same albedo regardless of material
assessmentOpportunities: Students rank surfaces by albedo and verify with the simulation
Students calculate additional energy absorbed when ice converts to ocean
Students propose urban design changes to reduce the heat island effect using albedo concepts
